name: Mock Mincemeat
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash, core, and chop unpeeled green tomatoes.
2. Grind the tomatoes using a food processor, food mill, or an old-fashioned sausage grinder.
3. Drain the tomatoes through a colander lined with cheesecloth into a pan.
4. Measure the juice after draining and discard it.
5. Place the tomatoes in a large enamel or stainless steel kettle. Add water equal to the amount of discarded tomato juice.
6. Bring the mixture to a boil, then remove from heat.
7. Drain the mixture again through the cheesecloth into a pan, discarding the juice.
8. Return the tomatoes to the kettle and add more water equal to the amount of water discarded.
9. Bring the mixture to a boil and repeat the draining process.
10. Return the tomatoes to the kettle and add the remaining water equal to the discarded tomato juice.
11. Wash, core, and chop apples.
12. Add the apples, raisins, vinegar, sugar, cinnamon, allspice, cloves, and salt to the tomato mixture.
13. Cook until thick (1 to 1 1/2 hours), stirring frequently.
Note: The mincemeat can be used as a pie filling for cakes, in cookies, or eaten as jelly on biscuits, English muffins, or toast. It also makes a great topping for ice cream. One quart of mincemeat is sufficient for a 9-inch pastry shell.
